_doc.what_this_is: Operator-tunable sizing dials. These are the knobs that decide the LIVE suggested position size on a BUY/ADD card. Conviction drives the size UP; every former hard cap is a dial here, defaulted OFF or generous. Nothing here auto-executes a trade -- the dashboard only SUGGESTS a size; the operator places every order by hand.
how_the_size_is_computed_plain_language: 1. Start from the proposed dollars the reallocation brief already put on the card (the existing anchor). If that is zero, fall back to base_size_usd.

2. Conviction LIFT. Only an HONEST, high-conviction, non-conflicted, BUY-aligned read lifts the size. The lift multiplier is: 1 + conviction_size_slope * conviction_strength, where conviction_strength is a 0..1 number built from the read band (HIGH=1.0, MODERATE=0.5, LOW/none=0.0), the 1..5 strength rung (scaled to 0..1), and whether the evidence converges across at least min_converging_groups independent groups. A name with no read, a conflicted read, a SELL/NEUTRAL read, or a single-group read gets NO lift (multiplier 1.0). This is the F1 honesty rail: size keys off the independence-collapsed, freshness-decayed conviction, never off raw echo or stale counts.

3. SOFT MAX dials (optional, default OFF). per_name_soft_max_usd and concentration_soft_max_pct are NOT hard clamps. When set and the lifted size exceeds them, the size is held at the soft max AND a visible note states the original lifted number and which dial bit. When null, they do nothing -- the conviction-driven size flows through unclamped.

4. CASH REALITY (informational, never a block). If a real available-cash / buying-power number exists in the account data, the card shows it and flags exceeds_cash when the suggested size is larger -- as a NUMBER, never a hidden block. When cash data is absent it shows not_checked. Nothing is silently reduced.

5. FUNDING REALITY (informational, never a block). The operator rarely holds cash; a buy is almost always funded by SELLING something else. So the honest affordability number is cash on hand PLUS the sellable funding pool from the reallocation brief (feed.reallocation_brief.funding.pool_total_usd -- already sell-gate-ordered: AI-factor wrappers first, broad reservoirs last, funding-protected wrappers excluded). When treat_funding_pool_as_available is true (default) the card adds the pool to cash to get funding_available_usd, and shows exceeds_funding / funding_shortfall_usd (max(0, suggested - funding_available)) plus a plain-language funding_note. This NEVER changes suggested_usd or the conviction lift -- it only ADDS the affordability number; the human decides.

6. ROUND (display precision, FINAL step). Round EVERY dollar amount the card shows -- suggested_usd plus the cash/funding fields available_cash, funding_pool_usd, funding_available_usd, funding_shortfall_usd, and the cap_room context dollar -- to the NEAREST size_rounding_usd (default $100), and store it as a WHOLE NUMBER so no decimal tail ever renders -- on the static card AND in the live client-side recompute when the operator drags the adjustable sizing bar (the render mirrors this exact formula, so it rounds identically). The covered/short booleans (exceeds_cash / exceeds_funding) are recomputed from the ROUNDED numbers so the displayed dollars and the verdict agree. Multipliers and percentages (size_lift_mult, size_lift_strength, ceiling_pct) are NOT dollars and are left untouched. This is display precision ONLY: it does NOT change the conviction lift, eligibility, the funding logic, or which names size up.

Former HARD caps now removed as limits and represented here as dials: the static TIER_BANDS ceiling, the concentration cap, the cap_room clamp, and the sell-gate-as-blocker are no longer hard stops. tier_ceiling_is_soft_reference=true means the tier ceiling is shown for context only and does NOT clip the size.
_doc.size_rounding_usd_doc: Display-precision dial for the FINAL step of the sizing formula. Every dollar amount the card shows is rounded to the nearest size_rounding_usd and stored as a whole number (int) so no long decimal tail ever renders -- the operator did not want decimals on the sizing numbers when dragging the adjustable size bar. Set 1 for whole dollars, 100 for the nearest hundred (default), or 1000 for the nearest thousand. A 0/None/negative value disables step rounding but the amount is still rounded to a whole dollar so no decimal ever shows. Round-to-nearest, e.g. 151890.48 -> 151900. This is display precision ONLY: it never changes the conviction lift, eligibility, the funding logic, or which names size up.
_doc.treat_funding_pool_as_available_doc: When true (default), the funding-aware affordability reality counts cash on hand PLUS the reallocation-brief sellable funding pool (pool_total_usd) as what could fund a suggested buy -- because the operator usually funds a buy by CONVERTING one ETF/stock to another, not from idle cash. When false, only literal cash on hand counts (funding_available_usd == cash). Either way it is informational only: never blocks, never clamps, never changes suggested_usd. The funding pool is REUSED from the reallocation brief, which is already sell-gate-ordered and excludes funding-protected / live-thesis-at-its-low names -- the sell-gate is NOT re-implemented here.
_doc.evidence_rail_note: This file tunes SIZING only. It contains no rail key that tunables.py forbids (those rails are non-tunable and live in tunables.py). A genuinely CONFLICTED card is still routed to RESOLVE/RECHECK and is never auto-sized-up -- that is enforced in the engine, not a dial you can turn off here.
